content_blocks.text:
## 8. Internal Control and Oversight

In accordance with Paragraph 3.6 of the 2026 FIU-IND Guidelines, the Company maintains internal controls and oversight mechanisms covering: (i) periodic risk assessment; (ii) risk classification of clients; and (iii) independent review of policies, controls, systems, procedures, and safeguards.

#### 8.1.5 Board Reporting (Paragraph 3.6.1(e))

The outcome of the Risk Assessment exercise and the mitigation measures identified and implemented shall be put up to the Board. The Risk Assessment documentation shall be made available to relevant authorities (including FIU-IND) as and when required.

### 8.2 Risk Classification of Clients

#### 8.2.1 Board-Approved Framework

In accordance with Paragraph 3.6.2 of the 2026 Guidelines, the Company maintains a Board-approved framework laying down principles for risk classification of its clients.

#### 8.2.2 Minimum Risk Categories (Paragraph 3.6.2(a))

Clients shall be classified at the minimum under two categories:

(a) **High Risk**

(b) **Medium Risk**

The Company may develop further categories over and above these two minimum categories for clients with higher risk perception (for example, an additional Low Risk category or a Prohibited category for relationships that cannot be onboarded).

The parameters and factors for determining risk perception under each category, including those relevant for suspicious transaction monitoring and application of enhanced measures under CDD, are clearly defined and objectively laid out in the Company's Risk Classification Framework, approved by the Board.

#### 8.3.5 Remediation

The Company shall develop a documented remediation plan in response to audit findings. The Principal Officer shall report remediation progress to the Board or Board sub-committee on a quarterly basis until all findings are closed.
